_description: Ground Truth knowledge base of classical titanium alloy material mechanisms, used to verify whether the DAG successfully recovers known metallurgical causal relationships

mechanisms.description: Alpha stabilizers (Al) and beta stabilizers (V) increase strength through solid solution strengthening

triads.mechanism_detail: Al forms substitutional solid solution in alpha-Ti; lattice distortion impedes dislocation motion
Solid solution strengthening effect increases ultimate tensile strength
V forms solid solution in beta-Ti and stabilizes the beta phase
V solid solution strengthening increases ultimate tensile strength

mechanisms.description: Grain refinement increases strength by increasing grain boundary area to impede dislocation motion

triads.mechanism_detail: Hall-Petch relationship: sigma_y = sigma_0 + k * d^(-1/2)
Fine grains increase grain boundary area, strengthening the Hall-Petch effect
Grain refinement strengthening increases both yield and ultimate tensile strength
Dynamic recrystallization during hot compression refines grains
Static recrystallization during annealing refines grains

mechanisms.description: Aging treatment precipitates second-phase particles such as Ti3Al (alpha2) or silicides that impede dislocation motion

triads.mechanism_detail: Aging treatment promotes second-phase precipitation from supersaturated solid solution
Heat treatment controls precipitate morphology and distribution
Precipitate particles impede dislocation motion and increase hardness
Strengthening via Orowan bypassing or shearing mechanism

mechanisms.description: Microstructure refinement and strengthening brought by beta to alpha/alpha' martensite phase transformation

triads.mechanism_detail: Rapid cooling from the beta phase region forms martensitic microstructure
Martensitic microstructure has high dislocation density and high hardness
Martensite phase transformation introduces high-density dislocations and refined microstructure, increasing strength
Solution treatment followed by rapid cooling forms martensite

mechanisms.description: Lamellar alpha microstructure increases fracture toughness through crack deflection mechanism

triads.mechanism_detail: Lamellar interfaces deflect cracks, increasing fracture energy dissipation
Lamellar alpha impedes dislocation climb during high-temperature creep

mechanisms.description: Equiaxed alpha microstructure provides better ductility and fatigue performance

triads.mechanism_detail: Equiaxed alpha grains provide more slip systems with good deformation compatibility
Equiaxed microstructure suppresses fatigue crack initiation

mechanisms.description: Regulation of alpha phase morphology by heat treatment and thermomechanical processing parameters

triads.mechanism_detail: Annealing in the alpha+beta phase region promotes alpha phase spheroidization to form equiaxed microstructure
Dynamic recrystallization during forging forms equiaxed alpha
Annealing in the beta phase region followed by slow cooling forms lamellar alpha microstructure
Dynamic spheroidization during hot compression

mechanisms.description: Special microstructure evolution in additive manufacturing processes such as DED/LPBF

triads.mechanism_detail: Rapid cooling in additive manufacturing forms acicular alpha' martensite
Extremely high cooling rate (~10^3-10^6 K/s) leads to martensite transformation
LPBF extremely high cooling rate forms fully martensitic microstructure
Microstructure refinement in additive manufacturing increases strength

mechanisms.description: Interstitial atoms (O/N/C) simultaneously increase strength and decrease ductility

triads.mechanism_detail: Oxygen atoms form interstitial solid solution in alpha-Ti, significantly strengthening it
Interstitial oxygen atoms restrict dislocation motion and reduce ductility
Carbon interstitial solid solution or TiC particle formation increases hardness

mechanisms.description: Orowan strengthening and grain refinement mechanism of TiB reinforcement phase in DED titanium alloys

triads.mechanism_detail: B reacts with Ti to in-situ form TiB whiskers/particles
TiB particles increase UTS through Orowan bypass strengthening
TiB pins grain boundaries and refines microstructure, Hall-Petch strengthening
